name: Check `n Go
description_short: Check `n Go Fort Worth, Texas — Check `n Go offers payday loans and installment loans ($100–$3,000) with same-day in-store funding, serving underbanked ...
description_long:
Check `n Go was founded in 1994 in Covington, Kentucky and has grown into one of the larger short-term consumer lenders in the United States. The company operates under CNG Holdings, Inc., a privately held entity and wholly owned subsidiary of CNG Financial Corporation, doing business through Axcess Financial Services. It is not a CDFI, HUD-approved agency, or NFCC-certified nonprofit counseling service. Check `n Go holds state lending licenses in multiple jurisdictions, including a California DFPI Debt Collector license (#10435-99), Delaware State Bank Commissioner approval through Eastern Specialty Finance, Inc., and oversight under Kentucky and Ohio financial regulators. It operates both a physical store network and an online lending platform across roughly 14 states.

The company's core products are payday loans (cash advances) and installment loans. Payday loans carry short repayment windows of 7 to 40 days and are repaid in a lump sum plus fees. Installment loans range from $100 to $3,000 (up to $5,000 in select states) with fixed repayment terms of 3 to 18 months, structured around the borrower's pay cycle. APRs are steep — ranging from approximately 190% to over 380% depending on the state, with specific examples including 260% in South Carolina, 360% in Delaware, and 370% in Missouri. An origination fee of $25 flat or 1%–3% of the loan amount applies. A late fee of 5% of the delinquent payment kicks in after 10 days. Check `n Go also offers the Netspend Prepaid Mastercard® for unbanked customers and publishes financial education content through its Finance Academy. No traditional credit score is required — the product explicitly targets consumers who may be turned away by conventional lenders.

What distinguishes Check `n Go operationally is its dual-channel model: same-day in-store funding for walk-in customers, and next-business-day funding via its online portal and mobile apps (available on both iOS and Android). Fixed installment payments aligned to pay schedules are a practical differentiator from lump-sum payday models. The Netspend prepaid card integration is a genuine value-add for the unbanked segment. The company has been operating for over 30 years, which gives it regulatory experience and geographic breadth that many smaller operators lack.

Honestly assessed, Check `n Go fills a real market gap for consumers with limited credit access, but it comes with significant cost. Triple-digit APRs — frequently above 300% — mean even short borrowing periods can become expensive quickly. The company is not BBB accredited, and its BBB standing is unverified at the national level, with some store profiles listed as closed. There is no money-back guarantee and no verified loan rescission marketing beyond standard state-mandated rights. Borrowers in states with strong consumer protections will see lower rates; those in permissive states can face some of the highest legal loan costs available. This is a last-resort or bridge product, not a wealth-building tool.
address: 6742 Forest Hill Dr, Fort Worth, TX 76140

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee offered. State law may provide a right to rescind within 1 business day, but this is not marketed by the company.

tiers.name: Installment Loan
features: Loan amounts from $100 to $3,000 (up to $5,000 in select states)
Payments aligned to borrower pay cycle
APR ranges from ~190% to 380%+ depending on state
Origination fee of $25 flat or 1%–3% of loan amount
Late fee of 5% after 10 days delinquent

tiers.name: Netspend Prepaid Mastercard
features: Designed for unbanked and underbanked customers

certifications: Licensed by Delaware State Bank Commissioner (Eastern Specialty Finance, Inc.)
Regulated by Kentucky Department of Financial Institutions
Regulated by Ohio Department of Commerce – Financial Institutions

pros: Founded in 1994 — over 30 years of short-term lending operations
Same-day in-store funding available for walk-in borrowers
Installment loans with fixed payments aligned to borrower pay cycle reduce payment shock
Available in 14 states both in-store and online
iOS and Android apps plus online account portal for self-service management
Offers Netspend Prepaid Mastercard for unbanked customers — rare among payday lenders
Finance Academy educational content available on website
cons: APRs range from ~190% to over 380% — among the highest legally permitted consumer loan rates
Not BBB accredited; national BBB rating is unverified with some store profiles listed as closed
No CDFI, HUD-approved, or NFCC certification — not a nonprofit or mission-driven lender
Loan amounts cap at $3,000 ($5,000 in select states) — inadequate for larger financial emergencies
Availability is limited to 14 states; borrowers in most of the U.S. cannot access their products
best_for: Underbanked consumers who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans or credit cards
Borrowers needing same-day cash access at a physical store location
Unbanked individuals who also need a prepaid debit card solution
People needing a short-term bridge loan of $100–$3,000 with no credit score requirement
diagnosis: Check `n Go is best suited for consumers with limited or no access to conventional credit who need fast, small-dollar cash — particularly those near one of their physical store locations where same-day funding is possible. The main caveat is cost: APRs routinely exceed 300%, making this a viable option only when the borrower has a clear, short-term repayment plan and no lower-cost alternatives available.
services: Payday loans (cash advances) — 7 to 40 day repayment
Installment loans — $100 to $3,000, 3 to 18 month terms
Netspend Prepaid Mastercard for unbanked customers
Online loan application and account management portal
iOS mobile app for account and loan management
Android mobile app for account and loan management
In-person store service at locations across 10 states
Online lending in 14 states including Alabama, California, and Texas
Money order services (per Google business classification)
